summary: Score-Pairwise Coherence feature fully tested and working. Backend API returns coherence data with status=ok, 3 models (claude, gpt, gemini), each with label, total_pairs, overall_agreement, and 6 bins. Frontend renders the coherence section with bar chart and data table correctly.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Score-Pairwise Coherence feature is complete and working. Key findings: (1) Backend API /api/model-analysis returns score_pairwise_coherence with status=ok, (2) Contains 3 models: claude (6323 pairs, 81% agreement), gpt (1079 pairs, 74% agreement), gemini (809 pairs, 78% agreement), (3) Each model has 6 bins with agreement rates that increase as score gap increases, (4) GPT has narrow score range with very few pairs at high gaps (known behavior), (5) Per-category filter works (tested with cs.RO), (6) Frontend renders CoherenceSection with bar chart and data table, (7) All data-testid attributes present: coherence-section, coherence-row-claude, coherence-row-gpt, coherence-row-gemini

test_cases: Each model has label, total_pairs > 0, overall_agreement in [0,1], 6 bins
Each bin has label, gap_min, gap_max, n, agreement_rate, reversal_rate
Agreement rate increases as score gap increases
Sum of bin counts equals total_pairs
Returns coherence data for specific category
Bar chart shows agreement rate by SI score gap
Data table with rows for all 3 models
17 chart bars rendered (3 models x 6 bins, minus empty bins)

gpt.note: GPT has narrow SI score range, very few pairs at high gaps
